channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta adds Trump ally and UFC boss Dana White to its board
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Zoox and Waymo are hoping Trump will ease barriers for self-driving cars
item.description: A group representing self-driving car companies on Tuesday called on the U.S. government to do more to speed the deployment of autonomous vehicles and remove barriers to adoption.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: US probing Teslas over driverless feature
item.description: The U.S. government is probing Tesla over a driverless feature in its electric vehicles, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: When he was still a boy making the long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia's Huang Open to Meeting Trump, Offering AI Guidance
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang expresses willingness to meet US President-elect Donald Trump and offer assistance on AI policy.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How Spotify is turning podcasting into social media
item.description: When Spotify introduced video podcasts, its interface started to look more like TikTok. Now the music streaming giant has added a glorified creator fund.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Meta looking for 'sweetheart deal' with Trump: FTC chair
item.description: Outgoing Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan suggested Tuesday that Meta and other companies facing antitrust lawsuits from the agency might be looking for “sweetheart deals” from the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft Accused of Underhand Bing Promotion - Tech News
item.description: Microsoft faces backlash over allegations it uses underhand tactics to promote its search engine, Bing, at the expense of Google.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Emirati billionaire investing $20B in US to build data centers
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Tuesday that Emirati billionaire Hussain Sajwani plans to invest $20 billion in the United States “over a very short period of time” to build data centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Gunnar Optiks reveals new lenses and gaming-themed frames at CES
item.description: The company that says its products helps with digital eye strain is offering new lenses including a electrochromic ones that changes tint instantly
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: This startup says its AI can speed insurance approval of health treatments
item.description: In recent years, doctors and patients have reported serious frustration with requirements from insurers for prior authorization before a laundry list of medical treatments and procedures can take place.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: The Last of Us season 2's new teaser has fans gushing over every detail it contains, but some are also concerned about the welfare of one of its new cast members
item.description: The Last of Us season 2's latest round of footage is full of moments lifted directly from Naughty Dog's video game franchise.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Maingear's newest PC builds are so good I wish I could buy them all, but I'll happily take the Miami Vice one, thanks
item.description: Maingear's latest custom-built gaming PCs are on display at CES 2025, and trust me, you're going to want to see them in person.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Until Dawn's movie adaptation won't follow the same story
item.description: Sony's Until Dawn movie won't be a direct adaptation of Supermassive Games' horror title of the same name.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: HMD OffGrid: Satellite Communication Device Launched at CES 2025 | Tech Radar
item.description: HMD has launched a new location-sharing device, the HMD OffGrid, which brings satellite communication capabilities to any smartphone. The device costs $199 / £169 upfront with a $14.99 / £14.99 monthly subscription fee.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia is jumping on the agentic AI bandwagon with new "blueprints" to simplify work
item.description: Nvidia wants to help businesses build agentic AI applications with new in-house and third-party blueprints.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Digital transformation 2025: What’s in, what’s out
item.description: Some argue gen AI’s emergence has rendered “digital transformation” passé. AI transformation is the term for them. Others suggest everything should be called “business transformation” — or just “transformation” for short.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Guzman Minerals se apoya en una infraestructura en la nube para una gestión centralizada y segura
item.description: Desde su fundación en el año 1939, el grupo empresarial de carácter familiar Guzmán Global ha estado dedicado al suministro de materias primas industriales, abarcando sectores como minerales, café, frutos secos e ingredientes para la industria alimentaria, tanto a nivel nacional como internacional.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Demand for AI skills soars, while demand for programming skills falls – O’Reilly report
item.description: It’s perhaps not a surprise, but demand for AI-related skills has soared, according to technology educator O’Reilly, which just released its annual technology trends report. By contrast, interest in programming languages Python and Java has taken a bit of a nosedive, the company reports.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World
